# pheno-errors

## Purpose
Canonical error primitives for the Phenotype fleet. Provides thiserror-derived error types, error context propagation, and structured error reporting across Rust + Python.

## When to use
- Building a new Rust library in the fleet
- Propagating errors across service boundaries
- Need typed error enums with backtraces

## When NOT to use
- HTTP-level error responses (use pheno-port-adapter for that)
- Logging-only error traces (use pheno-tracing)

## Quickstart
```rust
use pheno_errors::prelude::*;
fn parse(input: &str) -> Result<MyType, Error> {
    input.parse().map_err(|e: ParseIntError| Error::wrap(e))
}
```

## Env vars
None (no runtime config).

## Key types
- `Error` — wrapper enum (Internal, External, Invalid)
- `ErrorContext` — propagating context for `?`-style chains
- `Wrap<T>` — typed result with trace

## Example
See `pheno-errors/examples/wrap.rs` for the canonical wrap pattern.
